Category_Definition: Feedback about staff performance behavior and interpersonal interactions with reps agents associates or in-home experts across all service channels.
Topic_Definition: The rep's actual ability to help resolve or address the customer's need — taking action rather than just being pleasant.
Sub_Topic_Definition: Customer explicitly states the rep helped them assisted them or got things done. Include: 'very helpful' 'he helped me' 'she assisted' 'helped me out' 'helped me a lot' 'so helpful' 'extremely helpful' 'took care of everything' 'handled it' 'got it done for me'. Distinguish from general praise (Overall Positive) by presence of help/assist/resolve language.

Category_Definition: Feedback about staff performance behavior and interpersonal interactions with reps agents associates or in-home experts across all service channels.
Topic_Definition: The rep's actual ability to help resolve or address the customer's need — taking action rather than just being pleasant.
Sub_Topic_Definition: Customer states rep failed to help or was unable to assist despite interaction. Include: 'no help at all' 'wasn't helpful' 'didn't help me' 'couldn't assist' 'wasn't of any help' 'no help' 'wasn't able to help' 'useless' 'did nothing for me' 'waste of my time talking to them'. Exclude rude behavior (see Attitude) and knowledge gaps (see Knowledge).

Category_Definition: Feedback about staff performance behavior and interpersonal interactions with reps agents associates or in-home experts across all service channels.
Topic_Definition: The rep's actual ability to help resolve or address the customer's need — taking action rather than just being pleasant.
Sub_Topic_Definition: Customer indicates the rep exceeded expectations or did more than required. Include: 'went above and beyond' 'went the extra mile' 'did more than expected' 'exceeded expectations' 'bent over backwards' 'went out of their way' 'extraordinary effort' 'really went above and beyond for me'. Differentiate from standard helpfulness by the language of exceeding norms.

Category_Definition: Feedback about staff performance behavior and interpersonal interactions with reps agents associates or in-home experts across all service channels.
Topic_Definition: How the rep presented themselves interpersonally — tone warmth courtesy and respect toward the customer.
Sub_Topic_Definition: Customer describes rep as friendly kind warm nice pleasant sweet polite courteous wonderful lovely or delightful. Include: 'very friendly' 'so kind' 'very polite' 'nice attitude' 'pleasant voice' 'she was sweet' 'wonderful person' 'lovely woman' 'great personality'. Separate from helpful (Helpfulness topic) — this is about demeanor not action.

Category_Definition: Feedback about staff performance behavior and interpersonal interactions with reps agents associates or in-home experts across all service channels.
Topic_Definition: How the rep presented themselves interpersonally — tone warmth courtesy and respect toward the customer.
Sub_Topic_Definition: Customer describes rep as rude disrespectful nasty arrogant condescending dismissive mean snappy short or having an attitude. Include: 'she was rude' 'nasty attitude' 'very rude' 'disrespectful' 'heartless' 'arrogant' 'condescending' 'talked down to me' 'snappy' 'had an attitude'. High CX risk signal — escalation trigger.

Category_Definition: Feedback about staff performance behavior and interpersonal interactions with reps agents associates or in-home experts across all service channels.
Topic_Definition: How the rep presented themselves interpersonally — tone warmth courtesy and respect toward the customer.
Sub_Topic: Rep Hung Up / Ended Call Prematurely
Sub_Topic_Definition: Customer states rep hung up on them or disconnected the interaction without resolution. Include: 'hung up on me' 'she hung up' 'they disconnected me' 'he ended the call' 'I got hung up on' 'just hung up' 'terminated the call'. Distinct churn risk indicator. Exclude accidental technical drops (see Process — Call Dropped).

Category_Definition: Feedback about staff performance behavior and interpersonal interactions with reps agents associates or in-home experts across all service channels.
Topic_Definition: How the rep presented themselves interpersonally — tone warmth courtesy and respect toward the customer.
Sub_Topic: Rep Was Dismissive / Did Not Listen
Sub_Topic_Definition: Rep did not engage refused to listen was dismissive or felt robotic. Include: 'did not listen' 'wouldn't listen' 'didn't listen' 'didn't care' 'impersonal' 'robotic' 'he didn't care about my problem' 'ignored what I said' 'talked over me' 'wasn't interested in helping'. Exclude general unhelpfulness without dismissive language.

Category_Definition: Feedback about staff performance behavior and interpersonal interactions with reps agents associates or in-home experts across all service channels.
Topic_Definition: How the rep presented themselves interpersonally — tone warmth courtesy and respect toward the customer.
Sub_Topic: Rep Was Pushy / Aggressive Sales Approach
Sub_Topic_Definition: Rep was overly aggressive in selling or pushing products and services the customer did not want. Include: 'too pushy' 'kept trying to sell me' 'wouldn't take no' 'aggressive' 'hard sell' 'wouldn't stop pushing' 'forced products on me' 'pressured me to add services'. Distinct from helpful upsell — customer explicitly felt pressure.

Category_Definition: Feedback about staff performance behavior and interpersonal interactions with reps agents associates or in-home experts across all service channels.
Topic_Definition: The rep's conduct composure work ethic and professional standards during the interaction.
Sub_Topic_Definition: Customer uses professionalism language to describe the rep. Include: 'professional' 'very professional' 'professionalism' 'well spoken' 'composed' 'knowledgeable and professional' 'muy profesional' 'carried themselves well' 'business-like' 'proper'. High correlation with overall satisfaction.

Category_Definition: Feedback about staff performance behavior and interpersonal interactions with reps agents associates or in-home experts across all service channels.
Topic_Definition: The rep's conduct composure work ethic and professional standards during the interaction.
Sub_Topic_Definition: Customer calls out unprofessional behavior or perceived incompetence. Include: 'unprofessional' 'incompetent' 'total failure' 'doesn't know what they're doing' 'poor conduct' 'needs retraining' 'clearly not trained' 'sloppy' 'careless'. Actionable for coaching — distinct from rude (Attitude) by focus on competence vs. demeanor.

Category_Definition: Feedback about staff performance behavior and interpersonal interactions with reps agents associates or in-home experts across all service channels.
Topic_Definition: How well the rep understood the product gave accurate information and demonstrated expertise.
Sub_Topic_Definition: Customer indicates rep had strong product knowledge or gave accurate information. Include: 'knowledgeable' 'knew his stuff' 'very knowledgeable' 'knows the job' 'knew what they were doing' 'very informative' 'gave great info' 'well informed' 'knew the answer right away' 'expert'. Track for training effectiveness.

Category_Definition: Feedback about staff performance behavior and interpersonal interactions with reps agents associates or in-home experts across all service channels.
Topic_Definition: How well the rep understood the product gave accurate information and demonstrated expertise.
Sub_Topic: Rep Lacked Knowledge / Gave Wrong Information
Sub_Topic_Definition: Rep gave incorrect information seemed uninformed confused or was clearly guessing. Include: 'wrong response' 'didn't know' 'poor knowledge' 'guessing at problem' 'gave wrong info' 'didn't have a clue' 'still learning' 'not trained' 'gave me bad information' 'told me wrong' 'misinformation'. Exclude intentional deception (see Dishonesty).

Category_Definition: Feedback about staff performance behavior and interpersonal interactions with reps agents associates or in-home experts across all service channels.
Topic_Definition: How well the rep understood the product gave accurate information and demonstrated expertise.
Sub_Topic_Definition: Customer believes rep intentionally lied misled or deceived them about products pricing or account actions. Include: 'being lied to' 'I was lied to' 'she lied' 'misleading' 'he was not truthful' 'I was misguided' 'felt deceived' 'lied in contract' 'told me one thing did another' 'bait and switch'. High severity — flag for compliance investigation.

Category_Definition: Feedback about staff performance behavior and interpersonal interactions with reps agents associates or in-home experts across all service channels.
Topic_Definition: How well the rep understood the product gave accurate information and demonstrated expertise.
Sub_Topic_Definition: Customer reports getting different answers from different reps creating confusion. Include: 'every rep tells me something different' 'got different info each time' 'one rep said X another said Y' 'conflicting information' 'no one is on the same page' 'inconsistent answers'. Signals systemic knowledge base or training issue.

Category_Definition: Feedback about staff performance behavior and interpersonal interactions with reps agents associates or in-home experts across all service channels.
Topic_Definition: Whether the rep took adequate time listened carefully and was thorough in their approach.
Sub_Topic_Definition: Customer explicitly mentions patience or taking time. Include: 'very patient' 'he was patient' 'so patient' 'patient with me' 'took her time' 'took time to explain' 'patient person' 'never rushed me' 'gave me all the time I needed'. Strong satisfaction driver especially for elderly or non-technical customers.

Category_Definition: Feedback about staff performance behavior and interpersonal interactions with reps agents associates or in-home experts across all service channels.
Topic_Definition: Whether the rep took adequate time listened carefully and was thorough in their approach.
Sub_Topic_Definition: Rep gave thorough explanation was detail-oriented or reviewed everything completely. Include: 'very thorough' 'explained everything' 'went through everything' 'detailed' 'thorough walkthrough' 'covered all my questions' 'left no stone unturned' 'made sure I understood'. Combine with knowledgeable for training signal.

Category_Definition: Feedback about staff performance behavior and interpersonal interactions with reps agents associates or in-home experts across all service channels.
Topic_Definition: Whether the rep took adequate time listened carefully and was thorough in their approach.
Sub_Topic_Definition: Rep rushed the customer was impatient or made them feel hurried. Include: 'too fast' 'rushed me' 'didn't take time' 'felt hurried' 'was not patient' 'wanted to get me off the phone' 'seemed like they were in a rush' 'cut me short' 'didn't let me finish'. Exclude dismissive/rude (separate sub-topic).

Category_Definition: Feedback about staff performance behavior and interpersonal interactions with reps agents associates or in-home experts across all service channels.
Topic_Definition: How clearly the rep communicated — language clarity comprehension and ability to explain complex topics simply.
Sub_Topic: Rep Communicated Clearly / Easy to Understand
Sub_Topic_Definition: Customer praised rep's communication clarity or ability to explain. Include: 'clear explanation' 'easy to understand' 'well explained' 'communicated well' 'clear and concise' 'easy to talk to' 'bueno y claro' 'broke it down for me' 'made it simple' 'explained in plain language'. Track for bilingual/accent monitoring.

Category_Definition: Feedback about staff performance behavior and interpersonal interactions with reps agents associates or in-home experts across all service channels.
Topic_Definition: How clearly the rep communicated — language clarity comprehension and ability to explain complex topics simply.
Sub_Topic_Definition: Customer had difficulty understanding rep due to language accent or unclear speech. Include: 'hard to understand' 'couldn't understand' 'language barrier' 'accent was strong' 'speak clear English' 'speak English' 'can't understand him' 'heavy accent' 'broken English' 'mumbling'. Note: classify carefully — do NOT apply if customer is non-English speaking themselves.

Category_Definition: Feedback about staff performance behavior and interpersonal interactions with reps agents associates or in-home experts across all service channels.
Topic_Definition: How clearly the rep communicated — language clarity comprehension and ability to explain complex topics simply.
Sub_Topic_Definition: Customer explicitly requests domestic agents or English-speaking reps as a preference statement. Include: 'get some Americans' 'don't outsource' 'use local people' 'quit outsourcing' 'proper English' 'get correct answers' 'use American workers' 'bring jobs back'. Policy/ops signal — distinct from communication barrier by being a request rather than describing an experience.

Category_Definition: Feedback about staff performance behavior and interpersonal interactions with reps agents associates or in-home experts across all service channels.
Topic_Definition: How clearly the rep communicated — language clarity comprehension and ability to explain complex topics simply.
Sub_Topic: Rep Did Not Explain / Left Customer Confused
Sub_Topic_Definition: Rep failed to explain what was done what to expect next or left the customer without understanding. Include: 'didn't explain anything' 'left me confused' 'no explanation' 'didn't tell me what to expect' 'I don't know what happened' 'never explained the charges' 'still don't understand what was done'. Signals process/communication gap.

Category_Definition: Feedback about staff performance behavior and interpersonal interactions with reps agents associates or in-home experts across all service channels.
Topic_Definition: Whether the rep demonstrated genuine understanding of the customer's frustration situation or emotional state and took emotional ownership of the experience.
Sub_Topic_Definition: Customer felt the rep genuinely understood their frustration or situation. Include: 'understood my frustration' 'showed empathy' 'could tell she cared' 'understood my situation' 'made me feel heard' 'genuinely concerned' 'sympathetic' 'actually cared' 'took my concern seriously'. Distinct from patience — empathy is emotional acknowledgment not time spent.

Category_Definition: Feedback about staff performance behavior and interpersonal interactions with reps agents associates or in-home experts across all service channels.
Topic_Definition: Whether the rep demonstrated genuine understanding of the customer's frustration situation or emotional state and took emotional ownership of the experience.
Sub_Topic: Rep Lacked Empathy / Did Not Acknowledge Frustration
Sub_Topic_Definition: Rep showed no concern for the customer's emotional state or dismissed their frustration. Include: 'didn't care' 'no empathy' 'cold' 'couldn't care less' 'didn't acknowledge my frustration' 'no compassion' 'indifferent' 'treated me like a number' 'felt like I was bothering them'. Distinct from dismissive (Attitude) — this is absence of emotional connection specifically.

Category_Definition: Feedback about staff performance behavior and interpersonal interactions with reps agents associates or in-home experts across all service channels.
Topic_Definition: Whether the rep demonstrated genuine understanding of the customer's frustration situation or emotional state and took emotional ownership of the experience.
Sub_Topic_Definition: Rep apologized for the issue or experience and took emotional ownership even if they did not cause it. Include: 'apologized' 'said sorry' 'took responsibility' 'owned the mistake' 'acknowledged the error' 'admitted the issue' 'took the blame' 'said it shouldn't have happened'. Signals service recovery behavior.

Category_Definition: Feedback about staff performance behavior and interpersonal interactions with reps agents associates or in-home experts across all service channels.
Topic_Definition: Whether the rep demonstrated genuine understanding of the customer's frustration situation or emotional state and took emotional ownership of the experience.
Sub_Topic: Rep Did Not Apologize / Deflected Blame
Sub_Topic_Definition: Rep refused to apologize deflected blame to the customer or another department or made excuses. Include: 'no apology' 'blamed me' 'said it wasn't their fault' 'wouldn't take responsibility' 'passed the blame' 'deflected' 'made excuses' 'said nothing they could do'. Escalation risk — distinct from broken promise (Accountability).

Category_Definition: Feedback about staff performance behavior and interpersonal interactions with reps agents associates or in-home experts across all service channels.
Topic_Definition: Whether the rep did what they said they would do — kept promises followed up and took ownership of the resolution path.
Sub_Topic_Definition: Customer confirms the rep did what they said they would — called back applied credit completed action etc. Include: 'did exactly what they said' 'followed through' 'kept their word' 'called me back like they said' 'credit appeared' 'followed up' 'delivered on promise'. Strong trust-building signal.

Category_Definition: Feedback about staff performance behavior and interpersonal interactions with reps agents associates or in-home experts across all service channels.
Topic_Definition: Whether the rep did what they said they would do — kept promises followed up and took ownership of the resolution path.
Sub_Topic: Rep Did Not Follow Through / Broken Promise
Sub_Topic_Definition: Rep promised something and did not deliver — no callback no credit no resolution. Include: 'never called back' 'lied about callback' 'said they'd fix it and didn't' 'broken promise' 'never did what they said' 'promised a credit never got it' 'said they would call back' 'no follow up'. HIGH churn risk — trust violation.

Category_Definition: Feedback about staff performance behavior and interpersonal interactions with reps agents associates or in-home experts across all service channels.
Topic_Definition: Whether the rep did what they said they would do — kept promises followed up and took ownership of the resolution path.
Sub_Topic: Rep Took Ownership of the Issue
Sub_Topic_Definition: Rep took personal ownership of the problem and drove it to resolution rather than deflecting. Include: 'took ownership' 'made it their problem' 'personally handled it' 'didn't pass me off' 'stayed with me until resolved' 'owned it from start to finish' 'took charge'. Distinct from empathy — this is action-oriented ownership.

Category_Definition: Feedback about staff performance behavior and interpersonal interactions with reps agents associates or in-home experts across all service channels.
Topic_Definition: Whether the rep did what they said they would do — kept promises followed up and took ownership of the resolution path.
Sub_Topic: Rep Passed the Buck / No Ownership
Sub_Topic_Definition: Rep refused to own the problem sent customer elsewhere or said 'not my department'. Include: 'not my department' 'passed me off' 'no one takes ownership' 'just kept transferring' 'no one wanted to help' 'everyone points fingers' 'shuffled around' 'buck passing'. Overlaps with too many transfers but focuses on ownership refusal.

Category_Definition: Feedback about staff performance behavior and interpersonal interactions with reps agents associates or in-home experts across all service channels.
Topic_Definition: Customer expresses desire to formally recognize or retain a specific rep.
Sub_Topic_Definition: Customer recommends rep for raise promotion or special recognition. Include: 'give her a raise' 'give him a raise' 'give him a promotion' 'keep her' 'he's a keeper' 'she's a keeper' 'she's a superstar' 'she deserves a raise' 'promote her' 'best employee you have' 'employee of the month'. High satisfaction signal — retention opportunity.

Category_Definition: Feedback about staff performance behavior and interpersonal interactions with reps agents associates or in-home experts across all service channels.
Topic_Definition: Customer expresses desire to formally recognize or retain a specific rep.
Sub_Topic_Definition: Customer names a specific rep positively without raise/promotion language. Include: 'John was great' 'Maria was excellent' 'Sam was helpful' '[Name] did a great job' '[Name] is amazing'. Use for individual rep performance tracking. Extract name for supervisor recognition.

Category_Definition: Feedback about staff performance behavior and interpersonal interactions with reps agents associates or in-home experts across all service channels.
Topic_Definition: Customer expresses desire to formally recognize or retain a specific rep.
Sub_Topic_Definition: Customer names a specific rep negatively — complaint directed at an individual. Include: '[Name] was terrible' '[Name] was rude' '[Name] didn't help at all' 'fire [Name]' '[Name] needs training'. Use for individual coaching identification. Handle with HR sensitivity.

Category_Definition: Feedback about staff performance behavior and interpersonal interactions with reps agents associates or in-home experts across all service channels.
Topic_Definition: Holistic satisfaction or dissatisfaction with the rep not attributed to a specific behavior — use only when no specific behavior sub-topic applies.
Sub_Topic_Definition: General praise for the rep without a specific behavior anchor. Include: 'she was great' 'he was amazing' 'awesome rep' 'wonderful person' 'excellent employee' 'great service' 'she's awesome' 'he was fantastic' 'A+' '10 out of 10' 'couldn't ask for better'. ONLY use when no specific behavior sub-topic applies — prefer specific sub-topics.

Category_Definition: Feedback about staff performance behavior and interpersonal interactions with reps agents associates or in-home experts across all service channels.
Topic_Definition: Holistic satisfaction or dissatisfaction with the rep not attributed to a specific behavior — use only when no specific behavior sub-topic applies.
Sub_Topic_Definition: General negative about rep without specific behavior identifiable. Include: 'bad experience' 'total failure' 'horrible' 'waste of time' 'terrible rep' 'worst rep' 'awful experience' '0 stars' 'absolutely terrible'. Prefer specific sub-topics when behavior is identifiable — this is a catch-all.
